description:
You are helping a fellow script developer who wants to speed-up a very small utility that counts the total number of text-lines in a list of files.  
A deliberately slow reference implementation is already present on the system:

    /home/user/scripts/slow_linecount.sh

Your goals:

1.  Create a new directory:

        /home/user/tools

2.  Inside that directory write an optimized script named:

        /home/user/tools/fast_linecount.sh

    The optimized script **must**

    • start with the exact she-bang line  
      `#!/bin/bash`

    • perform the same job as the slow version, but without ever invoking the
      external command `cat`.  
      (Hint: use the direct form `wc -l "$file"` inside the loop.)

    • keep its total line-count in a shell variable called `total`
      and finally print that number to standard output (nothing else).

3.  Give the file execute permission so that it can be run directly.

4.  Verify that the new script gives identical results to the old one on the
   two sample data files that already exist in

        /home/user/data/file1.txt
        /home/user/data/file2.txt

5.  Produce a small log file that the automated checker can read:

        /home/user/tools/perf_check.log

   The file **must contain exactly three lines, in exactly this order**:

        slow_output=25000
        fast_output=25000
        optimized_script_uses_wc_directly=YES

   • There must be **no extra spaces**, leading or trailing.  
   • The two numeric values must be **decimal integers**.  
   • The third line must be the literal word **YES** (all capitals).

Hints (not mandatory):  
You can capture the script output via command substitution such as  
`slow=$( /home/user/scripts/slow_linecount.sh ... )`.

Expected end state that will be automatically verified:

• Directory `/home/user/tools` exists.  
• File `/home/user/tools/fast_linecount.sh` exists, is executable, contains the she-bang line, contains `wc -l` at least once, and does **not** contain `cat`.  
• File `/home/user/tools/perf_check.log` exists and matches the exact three-line format shown above.

Focus on accomplishing the above in as few commands as you comfortably can (3-4 commands are enough).  No root privileges are needed.
truth:
Pre-created files and their contents BEFORE the task starts:

1. /home/user/scripts/slow_linecount.sh  
------------------------------------------------  
#!/bin/bash  
# slow_linecount.sh: Count lines in files passed as arguments (slow variant)  
total=0  
for f in "$@"; do  
    lines=$(cat "$f" | wc -l)  
    total=$((total + lines))  
done  
echo "$total"  
------------------------------------------------  

2. /home/user/data/file1.txt – exactly 10 000 lines, each line contains the single word “dummy”.  
3. /home/user/data/file2.txt – exactly 15 000 lines, each line contains the single word “dummy”.

Verification checklist after agent finishes:

• /home/user/tools directory exists.  
• /home/user/tools/fast_linecount.sh is executable (mode 755 or at least user-executable).  
• fast_linecount.sh begins with “#!/bin/bash” (first line).  
• fast_linecount.sh contains the substring “wc -l” and does NOT contain the substring “cat”.  
• Running  
      /home/user/tools/fast_linecount.sh /home/user/data/file1.txt /home/user/data/file2.txt  
  outputs the single line “25000”.

• /home/user/tools/perf_check.log exists, is exactly three lines, byte-for-byte equal to:  
      slow_output=25000  
      fast_output=25000  
      optimized_script_uses_wc_directly=YES  

  (final newline at the end of the third line is acceptable but not required).

No additional files or directories should be created outside /home/user/tools.
